JONAS HECTOR ON BORUSSIA DORTMUND RADAR
Nemanja Lazarevic in Bundesliga, Transfer News & Rumours 18 Dec 2017

Borussia Dortmund have been showing interest in FC Koln full-back Jonas Hector, Sky Germany can reveal.

The Millionaires are reportedly not too happy with their current left-back options that include Marcel Schmelzer and Jeremy Toljan, and could make a move on the Germany international.

New Borussia Dortmund boss Peter Stoger knows Hector well from his time at FC Koln and he plans to get reunited with the 27-year-old at Signal-Iduna Park.

Sitting 11 points behind safety, the Billy Goats may be forced to sell some key men in the near future and they could find it hard to persuade the Germany star to stay.

Despite playing for FC Koln, Jonas Hector has been a first team regular in the national team for several years now, earning 35 caps.

Borussia Dortmund Winter Assessments: Summer Recruits 2017
by Navjot Kasukurthy40 minutes ago Follow @navjot4664

Winter is upon us, and the break is finally here. In a couple of weeks, the January Transfer Window will open and clubs will be clawing for any top-class talent that will enhance their squad. That’s just the nature of the transfer window. It brings about an excitement that is only matched by the actual game. As good as it is to look into the future, it always important to look behind and assess what was done. With that in mind, we will try to see who has and hasn’t done will amongst Dortmund’s summer recruits!

Jadon Sancho:

Joining from Machester City, the English winger has not been as important as fan’s would have hoped when he first arrived. The teenager has been reduced to the reserves due to certain technical issues. However, there is no doubting his ability. Sancho led the England U-17 in a sensational World Cup campaign in India. The speedy winger was a standout performer for the Three Lions and put any doubters to rest. With 14 goals in 18 appearances throughout the campaign, there are no apprehensions that Sancho will live up to the expectations. The left-winger is currently out injured with a stretched ligament, but will soon be available. He is also eligible for European competition from the 1st of January so that’s something to look forward to!

Dan-Axel Zagadou:

Another teenager on the list is France U-19 captain Dan-Axel Zagadou. The left-footed centre-back has played quite a bit this season in part due to injuries. The towering Zagadou occupied the left side of defence when Marcel Schmelzer suffered an injury spell. He has also deputised as a left-sided centre-back which is his ideal position. The former Paris Saint-Germain man has already racked up 11 appearances for the club and there are still many more to come. Unfortunately, his time with the club is already marred by a red card but it is not as bad as it looks. He is an incredible talent that BVB snatched up on a free which is a daylight robbery. Zagadou is easily one of the best teenage defenders on the planet at the moment and his composure will ensure he doesn’t fall off the wagon.

Jeremy Toljan:

Joining from TSG Hoffenheim, Toljan is a young and upcoming right-back who has been under-utilised this season. Another low-key signing, the 23-year-old was initially expected to displace Łukasz Piszczek on the right side of the defence. However, injuries forced Peter Bosz to try him on the left side in place of Marcel Schmelzer. He is a good talent but is yet to get accustomed to the club. So far, his performances have been uninspiring, to say the least. At best, he has looked like a decent option off the bench. It is BVB’s good fortune that Felix Passlack is still on the books.

Ömer Toprak:

Long-term target Toprak has little to no excuse. When he first arrived from Bayer Leverkusen, he was seen as a one-stop shop for all of Dortmund’s defensive problems. Since then, things have changed for the worse. Toprak looked good in his initial appearances but has started playing second fiddle to the more imposing Sokratis. Even with his skill on the ball and pace, Toprak gets caught out of position quite often and is lethargic. It’s not to say that he is a bad player. It’s just that he hasn’t impressed many during his time with the Black and Yellows.

Mahmoud Dahoud:

It disappoints me to talk about Dahoud. The midfielder is arguably the best young controlling midfielder in the game today. When he moved from Borussia Mönchengladbach, many, including myself, saw him as the direct replacement for the departing İlkay Gündoğan. And there are no two ways about it. Dahoud is fast, his passing is quick and precise. His footballing IQ is through the roof and his release of the ball is almost always on time. Yet, he has barely had any opportunities this season. Instead, players like Castro have had more opportunities than Dahoud. It would have been easy to call him a hit but the truth is, if you can’t play, you can’t be rated.

Maximilian Philip:

Maximilian Philipp is the future. This statement may be a little exaggerated, but there are no apprehensions in my mind about Philipp’s ability. The former SC Freiburg winger is another player bereft of opportunities. But such is his impact, that he manages to find the back of the net more often than not. Currently sidelined with injury, Philipp has to be the most productive of all the new recruits. It can also be argued that perhaps he is best utilised as a striker. However, only time will tell whether the German will eventually make that transition. For the time being, we can only hope he recovers to his best and starts banging in those goals again.

Andriy Yarmolenko:

Yarmolenko arrived with a lot of hype from Dynamo Kyiv. And initially, that hype was met with a lot of admiration. His deft touches and flicks added a flair to BVB’s style that had been missing for quite some time. However, Yarmolenko has pretty much been just that–flair. There is very little to his game apart from those skills. The winger is extremely one-footed and keeps missing shots that should at least be on target. What surprises me is that he has been better utilised as a second striker. When used centrally, the Ukrainian is able to feed Aubameyang with supply while evading tight defences. Yarmolenko is a good player but he will have to do more than being flashy to gain the favour of the fans.
